title: AP 4021 - AP 4021 (Part 2)
text:
Governing Board of TrusteesCollege CouncilIntegrated Planning and Budget CommitteeAdministrative Services CouncilInstruction CouncilStudent Services CouncilAcademic SenateAdministrative Support Management SenateAssociated Student BoardClassified SenateCurriculum CommitteeCOS Foundation Board of DirectorsMISSION STATEMENT: We are a comprehensive community college serving the economic, educational, lifelong learning, and workforce needs of Siskiyou County and the other communities our programs reach. College of the Siskiyous provides students a strong education leading to associate degrees, certificates, college transfer, career and technical education, workforce training, and basic skills preparation. We understand the transformative power of higher education and seek to inspire students by offering inclusive and practical learning experiences that are relevant in today’s global economy and environment. We strive to prepare our students to align their newly-gained skills and experiences with those best suited to the work they wish to pursue and the lives they wish to lead. At the same time, we aim to inspire our students to engage actively, question deeply, reason critically, think boldly, communicate effectively, act innovatively, work collaboratively, and live ethically. -- Adopted June 2019, Modified April 2021VISION STATEMENT: Transforming students' lives through lifelong learning and success. Diversity Statement, Mission, Vision, and Institutional Goals can be found here: https://www.siskiyous.edu/mainfiles/diversity_mission_vision.htm It is the policy of College of the Siskiyous not to discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex or disability in its educational programs and its employment practices. (https://www.siskiyous.edu/humanresources/nondiscrimination.htm and https://www.siskiyous.edu/mainfiles/titleIX.htm)________________________________________________________________________________________________________________For technical assistance with this site, please contact BoardDocs support at 800-407-0141, option 4 or support@boarddocs.com
For more information, please contact President's Office.

BookSiskiyou Joint Community College District Administrative Procedures
SectionChapter 4: Academic Affairs
TitleProgram Viability
CodeAP 4021
StatusActive
Last RevisedMay 7, 2025
Prior Revised Dates05/10/2016; 10/09/2012; 03/02/2010; 04/18/2000, 5/23/2019

title: AP 4021 - AP 4021 (Part 6)
text: In accordance with Title 5, Section 51022 and the recommendation of the Academic Senate for California Community Colleges, College of the Siskiyous has created procedures for assessing program viability and other actions that may result in program discontinuance. Definitions Educational program: an organized sequence of courses leading to a defined objective, a degree, a certificate, a diploma, a license, or transfer to another institution of higher education.Discipline: an individual area of study within a program for Associate Degree, Associate Degree for Transfer, or Certificate Programs.Department: A collection of associated disciplines and programs.Program Modification: Program modification as it relates to this process refers to a plan of action to enhance the performance and effectiveness of an existing program and/or department as part of a plan to avoid program discontinuance.Program Discontinuance: The termination of an existing Program, discipline, or Academic Department.Program Viability Review: The process of determining the appropriateness of a ProgramProgram Initiation Committee (sometimes referred to hereinafter as “Committee”): The standing committee established in consultation with the Academic Senate in accordance with this administrative procedure Program Initiation Committee Composition The Committee’s membership shall be: Faculty Chair (appointed by the Academic Senate President). Counselor (appointed by the Academic Senate President)Curriculum Committee Chair, or designee. Appropriate Dean or Vice-President A student representative appointed by the Associated Student Government, when available. If considering a program modification, then the committee shall include full-time faculty qualified to teach in the applicable program. PROCESS FOR PROGRAM VIABILITY - INITIATION: Proposals of Program Initiation are considered by the Program Initiation and Modification Committee, which then makes a recommendation to the Instruction Council and the Academic Senate. Proposals advocating the creation of a Program supported by grant funding, even in cases where the District has already obtained the grant, shall not be deemed approved, established, or initiated by default. Such proposals must also meet the evidentiary scrutiny established by this administrative procedure to obtain approval. Proposals for new programs shall address the following: The overall need for a new program and how that program aligns with state and local missions. Evaluation of institutional resources/statutory goals, which may include the need for and present adequacy of resourcesRecommendations of career technical education regional consortium, industry, and/or Program Advisory Committees, when applicableLabor market needs, if applicable Facilities and equipment required to sustain the programShort and Long-Term Staffing and Budgetary Plan, including a cost/revenue analysis Projected five-year plan that includes a projected course schedule and enrollment trends. Regional labor data, if applicable.

title: AP 4021 - AP 4021 (Part 7)
text: Comparison of the proposed program to similar programs within the regionComparison of the proposed program to similar programs within the regionExplanation of how the proposed program addresses an ongoing need not currently met by existing programs.Alignment of the Program to institutional outcomes and the strategic master planAbility of Programs to meet standards of external accrediting agencies, licensing boards, and governing bodies, if applicable. After reviewing a Program initiation proposal, the Program Initiation and Modification Committee shall make one of the following recommendations to the Instruction Council and the Academic Senate: Adopt proposal Decline the proposal PROCESS FOR PROGRAM VIABILITY – MODIFICATION Recommendations for program modifications, when necessary, shall occur through the Program Review process except when a recommendation to modify a Program is mandated by an external regulatory, governing, or licensing body to which the Program reports. In such cases, program modification shall occur as mandated. PROCESS FOR PROGRAM VIABILITY – DISCONTINUANCE In general, program discontinuance should be recommended only after a serious attempt has been made to improve program effectiveness and efficiency, and future efforts at remediation are not warranted. Program discontinuance is an academic and professional matter that requires consultation between the Board of Trustees and the Academic Senate. A consideration of program discontinuance is a process distinct from academic program review. Prior to submitting a proposal for program closure, there shall be a meeting with a representative of the Bargaining Unit, the discipline faculty involved (if applicable), and the appropriate Administrator. If the request moves forward, then a brief summary of the meeting should be documented in the proposal. The Program Discontinuance procedure allows for a comprehensive and systematic decision-making process in the event such an action is considered. Factors considered for Program Discontinuance include: Evaluation of institutional resources/statutory goals, which may includeThe need for and present adequacy of resourcesRecommendations of career technical education regional consortia and/or Program Advisory Committees, when applicableChanging labor market needsFacilities and equipment required to sustain the programAvailability of adequate financial support Availability of qualified faculty.Significantly modifying a program so that in effect one program has been discontinued and a new program createdLow student interest/completion rates. Proposals for Program Discontinuance will be considered as follows:The Office of Academic Affairs shall present the proposal for Program Discontinuance to the Instruction Council, which makes recommendations through the participatory governance structure to the Superintendent/President for recommendation to the Board of Trustees for final action. The Office of Academic Affairs shall present the proposal for Program Discontinuance to the Academic Senate for its recommendation to the Board of Trustees.

title: AP 4021 - AP 4021 (Part 8)
text: PROPOSAL GUIDELINES: The initial proposal to discontinue a program shall include, but is not limited to, the itemized quantitative and qualitative evidence listed in this section. Special attention shall be given to the impact of Program Discontinuance upon those students who are currently enrolled in the Program. Special attention shall also be given to the impact discontinuance has on existing programs and overall District functions. Quantitative Evidence - The quantitative evidence shall include (when applicable), but is not limited to, the following:Enrollment trends over the past five years Projected demand for the Program in the futureFrequency of course section offerings and/or rationale as to their reduction, if applicable Term-to-term persistence of students within the ProgramStudent success and Program completion ratesCurrent or projected student completion rateSuccess rate of students passing state and national licensing exams Data from Program ReviewCareer Education Considerations: Specific industry requests for this ProgramSupport from the Program Advisory Committee, as demonstrated through relevant data or feedback.Regional labor data that supports the proposal, when applicable.Analysis of any potential adverse student impact resulting from program discontinuance or transitionThe proposal shall substantiate adherence to standards of equity established by the State Chancellor’s Office. The program is compared to similar regional programs.This program meets an ongoing need that is not otherwise met, or capable of being met, by existing programs in the area. Qualitative Evidence - Factors to be considered shall include, but are not limited to: Analysis of the current relevance of a programCurrent curriculum offerings as they relate to the District’s academic mission Effect of Program Discontinuance on institutional outcomesImpacts on student equity Data from Program review, student evaluations, articulating universities, local businesses and/or industry, advisory committees, and community Value to the Division, college culture, and community at largeAbility of students to complete their degrees or certificates or to transfer. This includes maintaining the rights of students as stipulated in the District catalog Consideration of matters of articulation as they relate to the curriculum Existence of Programs in the surrounding community college districts Ability of Programs to meet standards of external accrediting agencies, licensing boards, and governing bodies Relation of the proposal to the District’s goals and strategies as outlined in the most recent Strategic and Master Plan Sustainable Budget Evidence. All proposals shall include a multi-year projected budgetary estimate and analysis, as well as a cost/revenue analysis for the preceding five years. Such analysis shall illustrate cost and revenue predictions and include a written narrative indicating how the proposal will be fiscally sustainable. Discontinuance Implementation Plan must include:A plan and timeline for implementing the Program Discontinuance.

title: AP 4021 - AP 4021 (Part 9)
text: A set of procedures to allow currently enrolled students to complete their Programs of study in accordance with the rights of students as stipulated in the District catalog. If Program completion is not viable, other equitable considerations must be accorded to students.A plan for the implementation of all affected collected bargaining requirements and matters for faculty and staff coordinating Program Discontinuance to be consistent with the District catalogue. Incomplete Proposals: Instruction Council or the Academic Senate may return proposals it deems incomplete due to insufficient supporting evidence to the proposing party. Mandated Program Discontinuance. A recommendation to discontinue a Program is mandated if ordered by an external regulatory, governing, or licensing body to which the Program reports. If such a mandate occurs, Program Discontinuance will be deemed approved upon the District’s proper notification of the Academic Senate. Such notification should clearly cite the governing entity and legal or administrative authority requiring Program Discontinuance. Academic Senate Action. The President of the Academic Senate shall place the Office of Academic Affairs recommendations on the Academic Senate agenda. The Academic Senate’s recommendation will then be forwarded to the Superintendent/President to be submitted to the Board of Trustees for approval. The Board of Trustees shall communicate, in writing, its reasons for rejecting any recommendations made pursuant to this administrative procedure. This Administrative Procedure was reviewed and approved by Superintendent/President Dr. Char Perlas on May 7, 2025.
